note: Project-scoped. Trust/disclosure patterns expected of consumer-finance UIs. Jurisdiction-specific regulation (PSD3/PSR, Consumer Duty, US state law) is confirmed by WRAI per-tenant, NEVER hard-coded — it is geography-bound and changes faster than any seed. Most rows route to the judge (copy/coherence).

standards.rule: Disclose fees and total cost at the decision point; the confirmation screen shows the full amount before commit.
standards.source_title: Fintech UX: fee transparency at point of decision

standards.rule: Use plain-language consent and disclosure; replace legalese with an expandable, readable explanation.
Progressive disclosure: lead with one primary number (e.g. balance) and reveal detail on intent.
Friction-right security: visible confirmation plus a receipt for money movement; never a silent success.
standards.source_title: Fintech UX: confirmation + receipt for money movement

standards.rule: Honest failure/outage status messaging (DORA, applies Jan 2025) — mirrors the agent always-acknowledge-degradation rule.
No cognitive-test-only login (cross-cutting WCAG 3.3.8 Accessible Authentication).
Jurisdiction-specific regulation (PSD3/PSR, Consumer Duty, US state) MUST be confirmed by WRAI per-tenant, never hard-coded.
